questions.question: She ___ (play) tennis every Sunday.
questions.explanation: Usamos 'plays' porque es tercera persona singular (she) en presente simple.

questions.question: They ___ (work) in a hospital.
questions.explanation: Con 'they' usamos el verbo base sin 's' en presente simple.

questions.question: The sun ___ (rise) in the east.
questions.explanation: Para verdades universales usamos presente simple. 'The sun' es singular.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: En negativo usamos 'doesn't' + verbo base (sin 's').

questions.question: ___ you ___ (speak) Spanish?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas con 'you' usamos 'Do' + verbo base.

questions.question: My mother ___ (cook) dinner every night.
questions.explanation: Con tercera persona singular (my mother) añadimos 's' al verbo.

questions.question: Water ___ (boil) at 100 degrees Celsius.
questions.explanation: Hechos científicos se expresan en presente simple.

questions.question: I ___ (not/like) spicy food.
questions.explanation: Con 'I' usamos 'don't' para formar el negativo.

questions.question: Select the correct Present Simple sentence:
options: She go to school by bus.
She goes to school by bus.
She going to school by bus.
She is go to school by bus.
questions.explanation: Con 'she' el verbo lleva 's': goes.

questions.question: The train ___ (leave) at 8:00 AM every day.
questions.explanation: Horarios fijos se expresan en presente simple.

questions.question: Look! The baby ___ (sleep) peacefully.
questions.explanation: 'Look!' indica que algo está pasando ahora, usamos presente continuo.

questions.question: They ___ (not/watch) TV right now.
questions.explanation: Con 'they' usamos 'aren't' + verbo-ing para negativo en presente continuo.

questions.question: I ___ (learn) English at the moment.
questions.explanation: 'At the moment' indica acción en progreso, usamos 'am' + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Which sentence uses Present Continuous correctly?
options: She is works at home today.
She is working at home today.
She are working at home today.
questions.explanation: Con 'she' usamos 'is' + verbo-ing.

questions.question: ___ he ___ (study) for the exam?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas en presente continuo: Is/Are + sujeto + verbo-ing.

questions.question: The children ___ (play) in the garden now.
questions.explanation: Con 'the children' (plural) usamos 'are' + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Why ___ you ___ (cry)?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas con 'you' en presente continuo: are + you + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Listen! Someone ___ (knock) on the door.
questions.explanation: 'Listen!' indica una acción ocurriendo ahora mismo.

questions.question: Which word signals Present Continuous?
questions.explanation: 'Right now' indica el momento presente, requiere presente continuo.

questions.question: My sister ___ (not/come) to the party tonight.
questions.explanation: Planes futuros cercanos también usan presente continuo.

questions.question: She ___ (finish) her homework already.
questions.explanation: 'Already' se usa comúnmente con presente perfecto. Con 'she' usamos 'has'.

questions.question: I ___ never ___ (be) to Japan.
questions.explanation: Con 'I' usamos 'have'. 'Been' es el participio pasado de 'be'.

questions.question: They ___ (live) here since 2010.
questions.explanation: 'Since' indica un punto en el tiempo y requiere presente perfecto.

questions.question: Which is the correct Present Perfect form?
options: He has went to the store.
He has gone to the store.
He have gone to the store.
He is gone to the store.
questions.explanation: 'Gone' es el participio pasado correcto de 'go'. Con 'he' usamos 'has'.

questions.question: ___ you ever ___ (try) sushi?
questions.explanation: 'Ever' en preguntas sobre experiencias de vida usa presente perfecto.

questions.question: We ___ (know) each other for 10 years.
questions.explanation: 'For' + período de tiempo requiere presente perfecto.

questions.question: The movie ___ just ___ (start).
questions.explanation: 'Just' indica algo que acaba de ocurrir, usamos presente perfecto.

questions.question: I ___ (not/see) that film yet.
questions.explanation: 'Yet' en oraciones negativas usa presente perfecto: haven't + participio.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: 'Written' es el participio pasado correcto de 'write'.

questions.question: My parents ___ (be) married for 25 years.
questions.explanation: Estado que continúa hasta ahora usa presente perfecto.

questions.question: She ___ (study) for 3 hours.
questions.explanation: 'For 3 hours' indica duración, presente perfecto continuo enfatiza la acción en progreso.

questions.question: I ___ (wait) here since 2 o'clock.
questions.explanation: 'Since' con acciones que continúan hasta ahora usa presente perfecto continuo.

questions.question: It ___ (rain) all day.
questions.explanation: 'All day' sugiere una acción continua hasta el presente.

questions.question: Which sentence shows an action continuing until now?
options: They have been working here for 5 years.
They are working here for 5 years.
questions.explanation: Presente perfecto continuo muestra que la acción empezó en el pasado y continúa.

questions.question: How long ___ you ___ (learn) English?
questions.explanation: 'How long' pregunta por duración, usamos presente perfecto continuo.

questions.question: My eyes hurt because I ___ (read) all day.
questions.explanation: El resultado presente (ojos duelen) es causado por una acción continua.

questions.question: He ___ (run). That's why he's sweating.
questions.explanation: La evidencia presente (sudor) indica una acción reciente y continua.

questions.question: We ___ (not/sleep) well lately.
questions.explanation: 'Lately' indica un período reciente hasta ahora, usa presente perfecto continuo.

questions.question: Which emphasizes duration more?
options: I have lived here for 5 years.
I have been living here for 5 years.
I am living here for 5 years.
questions.explanation: El presente perfecto continuo enfatiza más la duración de la acción.

questions.question: The dog is tired because it ___ (play) in the park.
questions.explanation: El cansancio es resultado de una actividad reciente y continua.

questions.question: She ___ (go) to Paris last summer.
questions.explanation: 'Last summer' es un tiempo específico en el pasado, usamos pasado simple.

questions.question: They ___ (not/see) the movie yesterday.
questions.explanation: Para negativo en pasado simple usamos 'didn't' + verbo base.

questions.question: I ___ (buy) this car in 2020.
questions.explanation: 'In 2020' es un año específico, usamos pasado simple.

questions.question: Which sentence is in Past Simple?
questions.explanation: 'Ate' es el pasado simple de 'eat'.

questions.question: ___ you ___ (meet) her at the party?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas en pasado simple: Did + sujeto + verbo base.

questions.question: We ___ (visit) our grandparents last weekend.
questions.explanation: 'Last weekend' indica un tiempo pasado específico.

questions.question: The concert ___ (start) at 8 PM.
questions.explanation: Evento completado en el pasado usa pasado simple.

questions.question: She ___ (not/call) me back.
questions.explanation: Acción pasada completada usa 'didn't' + verbo base.

questions.question: Which word signals Past Simple?
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' es un marcador de tiempo pasado definitivo.

questions.question: The dog ___ (bark) all night long.
questions.explanation: Acción completada en un período pasado usa pasado simple.

questions.question: I ___ (read) a book when he called.
questions.explanation: Acción en progreso interrumpida por otra acción usa pasado continuo.

questions.question: They ___ (play) football at 5 PM yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'At 5 PM yesterday' indica una acción en progreso en un momento específico del pasado.

questions.question: While she ___ (cook), the phone rang.
questions.explanation: 'While' indica una acción en progreso cuando otra acción la interrumpe.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses Past Continuous?
options: She was slept when I arrived.
She was sleeping when I arrived.
She were sleeping when I arrived.
questions.explanation: Con 'she' usamos 'was' + verbo-ing.

questions.question: ___ you ___ (watch) TV at 8 PM last night?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas en pasado continuo: Was/Were + sujeto + verbo-ing.

questions.question: The birds ___ (sing) when the sun rose.
questions.explanation: Acción en progreso cuando otra ocurre usa pasado continuo.

questions.question: What ___ you ___ (do) at midnight?
questions.explanation: Pregunta sobre acción en progreso en momento pasado específico.

questions.question: I ___ (not/listen) when the teacher explained.
questions.explanation: Negativo del pasado continuo: wasn't/weren't + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Which describes two simultaneous past actions?
options: He was studying while she was cooking.
He has studied while she cooked.
questions.explanation: Dos acciones paralelas en el pasado usan pasado continuo.

questions.question: The baby ___ (cry) when we arrived home.
questions.explanation: Acción en progreso al momento de llegar usa pasado continuo.

questions.question: She ___ (leave) before I arrived.
questions.explanation: Para acciones que ocurrieron antes de otra acción pasada usamos past perfect.

questions.question: I ___ never ___ (see) such a beautiful sunset before that day.
questions.explanation: 'Before that day' indica una acción anterior a un punto en el pasado.

questions.question: By the time we got there, the movie ___ (start).
questions.explanation: 'By the time' + pasado simple requiere past perfect para la acción anterior.

questions.question: Which sentence shows an action before another past action?
options: He has eaten when he arrived.
He had eaten before he arrived.
He was eating when he arrived.
questions.explanation: 'Had eaten' muestra que comer ocurrió antes de llegar.

questions.question: They ___ (live) in London before they moved to Paris.
questions.explanation: Vivir en Londres ocurrió antes de mudarse, usamos past perfect.

questions.question: The train ___ already ___ (leave) when I reached the station.
questions.explanation: El tren partió antes de que llegara, acción anterior usa past perfect.

questions.question: She was sad because she ___ (fail) the exam.
questions.explanation: La razón de su tristeza (fallar) ocurrió antes de estar triste.

questions.question: I ___ (not/eat) anything, so I was very hungry.
questions.explanation: No comer ocurrió antes de tener hambre, usa past perfect.

questions.question: Which is correct?
options: After he had finished, he left.
After he has finished, he left.
questions.explanation: 'After' + past perfect muestra la secuencia correcta de eventos.

questions.question: We ___ (meet) before, but I couldn't remember where.
questions.explanation: El encuentro previo ocurrió antes del momento de recordar.

questions.question: She ___ (work) for 2 hours before she took a break.
questions.explanation: Acción en progreso antes de otra acción pasada usa past perfect continuous.

questions.question: They ___ (wait) since morning when the bus finally came.
questions.explanation: Enfatiza la duración de una acción antes de otro evento pasado.

questions.question: I ___ (study) all night, so I was very tired.
questions.explanation: La fatiga resulta de estudiar antes, usamos past perfect continuous.

questions.question: Which sentence emphasizes duration before a past event?
options: He ran for an hour yesterday.
He had been running for an hour when it started raining.
He was running when it rained.
He has been running for an hour.
questions.explanation: 'Had been running' muestra duración antes de que empezara a llover.

questions.question: How long ___ you ___ (drive) before the accident?
questions.explanation: Pregunta por duración antes de un evento pasado.

questions.question: Her eyes were red because she ___ (cry).
questions.explanation: Los ojos rojos son evidencia de llorar antes, past perfect continuous.

questions.question: The ground was wet. It ___ (rain).
questions.explanation: El suelo mojado indica que había estado lloviendo antes.

questions.question: We ___ (not/wait) long when the taxi arrived.
questions.explanation: Negativo del past perfect continuous: hadn't been + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Which shows an action causing a past result?
options: He was tired because he worked.
He was tired because he had been working.
He is tired because he works.
He was tired because he has worked.
questions.explanation: Past perfect continuous conecta la causa con el resultado pasado.

questions.question: The children were dirty. They ___ (play) in the mud.
questions.explanation: La suciedad indica que habían estado jugando antes.

questions.question: I ___ (help) you with your homework.
questions.explanation: Para promesas o decisiones espontáneas usamos 'will' + verbo base.

questions.question: She ___ (not/come) to the party tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'Won't' es la forma negativa de 'will'.

questions.question: I think it ___ (rain) later.
questions.explanation: 'I think' + predicción usa futuro simple con 'will'.

questions.question: Which sentence expresses a spontaneous decision?
questions.explanation: 'I'll' se usa para decisiones tomadas en el momento.

questions.question: ___ you ___ (be) at the meeting tomorrow?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas sobre el futuro: Will + sujeto + verbo base.

questions.question: Don't worry, everything ___ (be) fine.
questions.explanation: Promesa o tranquilización sobre el futuro usa 'will'.

questions.question: I promise I ___ (call) you tonight.
questions.explanation: Promesas usan 'will' + verbo base.

questions.question: The phone is ringing. I ___ (get) it.
questions.explanation: Decisión instantánea al momento usa 'will'.

questions.question: Which expresses a prediction?
questions.explanation: 'Will probably' expresa una predicción sobre el futuro.

questions.question: I ___ (not/tell) anyone your secret.
questions.explanation: Promesa negativa usa 'won't' (will not).

questions.question: At 8 PM tomorrow, I ___ (watch) the game.
questions.explanation: Para acciones en progreso en un momento específico del futuro.

questions.question: This time next week, she ___ (fly) to London.
questions.explanation: 'This time next week' indica un momento específico en el futuro.

questions.question: They ___ (not/work) at midnight.
questions.explanation: Negativo de futuro continuo: won't be + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Which sentence describes an action in progress at a future time?
questions.explanation: 'Will be finishing' muestra la acción en progreso a las 5.

questions.question: ___ you ___ (use) the car at 3 PM?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas en futuro continuo: Will + sujeto + be + verbo-ing.

questions.question: At noon tomorrow, we ___ (have) lunch.
questions.explanation: 'At noon tomorrow' indica momento específico futuro.

questions.question: Don't call at 9. I ___ (sleep).
questions.explanation: Acción en progreso a una hora específica futura.

questions.question: This time tomorrow, the students ___ (take) their exams.
questions.explanation: 'This time tomorrow' requiere futuro continuo.

questions.question: Which is correct for an ongoing future action?
questions.explanation: Futuro continuo describe acción en progreso en momento futuro.

questions.question: What ___ you ___ (do) this time next month?
questions.explanation: Pregunta sobre actividad en progreso en el futuro.

questions.question: By next year, I ___ (finish) my degree.
questions.explanation: 'By next year' indica que la acción estará completa antes de ese punto.

questions.question: She ___ (leave) by the time you arrive.
questions.explanation: La acción de irse estará completa antes de que llegues.

questions.question: By 2030, they ___ (live) here for 20 years.
questions.explanation: Para duración que estará completa en un punto futuro.

questions.question: Which sentence shows a completed action before a future time?
options: I will be reading the book.
I will have read the book by Friday.
questions.explanation: 'Will have read' indica que la lectura estará terminada antes del viernes.

questions.question: ___ you ___ (complete) the project by Monday?
questions.explanation: Para preguntas: Will + sujeto + have + participio pasado.

questions.question: By the end of this month, I ___ (save) $1000.
questions.explanation: 'By the end of' indica completar antes de ese momento.

questions.question: The movie ___ (start) by the time we get there.
questions.explanation: La película habrá empezado antes de llegar.

questions.question: By tomorrow, she ___ (not/finish) the book.
questions.explanation: Negativo futuro perfecto: won't have + participio.

questions.question: Which is correct?
options: By 6 PM, I will have eaten.
questions.explanation: 'By' + tiempo futuro requiere futuro perfecto.

questions.question: By next summer, we ___ (be) married for 10 years.
questions.explanation: Estado que se completará en un punto futuro.

questions.question: By December, I ___ (work) here for 5 years.
questions.explanation: Enfatiza la duración de una acción que continúa hasta un punto futuro.

questions.question: She ___ (teach) for 10 years by next June.
questions.explanation: 'By next June' indica un punto futuro donde la duración estará completa.

questions.question: By 9 PM, they ___ (drive) for 6 hours.
questions.explanation: Enfatiza que habrán estado conduciendo durante 6 horas.

questions.question: Which sentence emphasizes the duration of an action up to a future point?
options: I will have studied for 3 hours.
I will have been studying for 3 hours by noon.
questions.explanation: 'Will have been studying' enfatiza la duración continua hasta el mediodía.

questions.question: How long ___ you ___ (wait) by the time the bus arrives?
questions.explanation: Pregunta por la duración continua hasta el momento de llegada.

questions.question: By the time he retires, he ___ (work) for 40 years.
questions.explanation: Duración continua hasta el momento de jubilación.

questions.question: By next month, we ___ (live) here for a year.
questions.explanation: Enfatiza la duración continua de vivir aquí.

questions.question: She ___ (not/sleep) long when the alarm rings.
questions.explanation: Negativo: won't have been + verbo-ing.

questions.question: Which emphasizes ongoing duration until a future point?
options: By midnight, I will work for 12 hours.
By midnight, I will be working for 12 hours.
By midnight, I will have been working for 12 hours.
By midnight, I work for 12 hours.
questions.explanation: Futuro perfecto continuo enfatiza la duración continua.

questions.question: By the end of this year, he ___ (learn) English for 3 years.
questions.explanation: Duración del aprendizaje hasta fin de año.
